mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.4% as of Jan 2019 reflects recent Fed pivot from tightening
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% as of Jan 2019 near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that portends lower corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 505826M and HOUST at 1256K as of Dec 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over a 2-month window signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deceleration in payroll employment growth and rising inflation-adjusted wage pressure signals labor market tightness that increases corporate labor costs and reduces profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth < 0.1% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150574K and CPIAUCSL at 252.67 as of Jan 2019
regime_hint: late-cycle wage-price spiral or stagflationary pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly declines below 2% annualized rate, signaling sub-trend economic expansion that reduces earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.0% in most recent quarter
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of sub-2% growth quarters, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% while inflation-adjusted real rates remain positive, monetary policy is restrictive and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.4% and CPIAUCSL at 252.67 as of Jan 2019
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with positive real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of restrictive policy instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic models over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in housing starts below 1200K annualized units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking investors reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256K as of Nov 2018, near threshold
regime_hint: housing-led demand slowdown in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST < 1200 threshold are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
